comment: Uses residual connections and learning rate scheduling. Large dataset. Must be run on powerfull GPU such as a A100. Train maybe 100 epochs to converge.
_num_samples: will create a really large dataset.
_im_size: Resolution of the magnetic field grid (24x24)
_nNeutrons: Doubled to maintain spatial sampling density with the larger grid
_nAngles: Number of projection angles. (Can increase to 720 if disk space permits)
_wavelengths: Range and step size for neutron wavelengths in Angstroms
_scaleD: Geometric scaling factor for source/detector geometry
_minmax_B: Maximum magnitude of the generated magnetic field (Tesla)
_seed: Random seed for reproducible data generation
_out_dir: Output directory for the generated datasets
_batch_size: Data Generation: Runs 16 samples simultaneously on the GPU for maximum speed
_train_epochs: Training: Increased epochs to allow convergence on the larger grid
_train_batch_size: Training: Kept at 8 to prevent overwhelming the 32GB system RAM during DataLoader prefetching
_train_lr: Training: Lowered learning rate for stability with huge parameter counts
_hidden_dim: Training: Massive dense layer to fully utilize A100 and prevent information bottlenecks
_pool_res: Training: Expanded spatial pooling resolution to capture high-res features from 512 neutrons
_legacy: Compatibility: Set to false to leverage the multi-stage GPU-optimized architecture for the A100
_use_residual: Training: Enable a skip connection on the refiner so the CNN only learns to sharpen the image
_use_scheduler: Training: Reduce learning rate on loss plateau to ensure fine-grained convergence
_train_split: Training: Fraction of data used for training vs validation
